message:
test(frankenpandas): add MultiIndex Operations integration test (br-v33s4 / fd90.158)

fd90.158: continuing the README → integration-test coverage pattern
(fd90.146 / fd90.156 / fd90.157). The README's 'MultiIndex Operations'
example (lines 1383-1395) wasn't exercised by any integration test.
MultiIndex + IndexLabel were in the prelude (fd90.121 / fd90.128) but
no test verified the documented standalone-MultiIndex API chain.

Fix: add 'readme_multiindex_operations_compiles_and_runs' which
mirrors the README example. Builds 2 regions × 2 years = 4-entry
MultiIndex via from_product + set_names, extracts level 0, and
flattens with a separator. Verifies:
  - MultiIndex::from_product(Vec<Vec<IndexLabel>>) via .into() blanket
    on '\"east\".into()' (Utf8) and '2023i64.into()' (Int64)
  - .set_names(Vec<Option<String>>) consumes-self chain
  - .nlevels() / .len() introspection
  - .get_level_values(usize) returning Result<Index>
  - .to_flat_index(&str) returning a flat Index with composite labels

Skipped df.to_multi_index() from line 1398 — already exercised by
fp-frame's own tests; we focus on the standalone API surface.

Verified:
- cargo test -p frankenpandas — 7 lib + 5 integration + 2 doctests
  all pass

Pure additive test. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
